topic.title: Help Center
topic.summary: Practical guidance for attorneys, paralegals, and legal staff using Controversies for daily case and document work.
topic.keywords: help center onboarding quick start guides workflows legal teams tasks facts threads permissions token markup
topic.content:
Start Here

Use the topic browser on the left to move between guides, or use search to filter by workflow, screen, or API term.

  • Getting Started (Legal Team): first-day setup for legal teams.
  • Case Workflow Guide: intake-to-output sequence.
  • Form Assembly Guide: template and quality-control standards.
  • Conflict Checks Guide: conflicts.xml scanning, review, and management.
  • Facts Guide (Novice) and Facts Guide (Expert).
  • Tasks Guide (Novice) and Tasks Guide (Expert).
  • Business Processes (Expert) for shared legal BPM actions.
  • Threads Guide (Novice) and Threads Guide (Expert).
  • Permissions Guide for layered access controls.

Token + Markup Reference

  • Token Guide
  • Markup Notation

Common Legal Team Tasks

Task Primary Screen When To Use It
Open a new matter Cases At intake, when a client matter is accepted.
Capture matter facts Facts Case Plan When structuring claims/elements/facts and linking evidence sources.
Track work assignments and deadlines Tasks For due-date-driven work, sub-task planning, and assignment/reassignment history.
Maintain reusable firm values Tenant Fields For values reused across many matters (firm, venue defaults, signature blocks).
Assemble a filing draft Form Assembly When generating a matter-specific document from a template.
Review generated outputs Assembled Forms To download, verify, and manage completed assembled files.
Confirm audit history Activity Logs For internal review, troubleshooting, or compliance checks.

Quick Tips For New Users

  • Use consistent naming for cases and templates to make retrieval faster under deadline pressure.
  • Prefer explicit tokens such as {{case.county}} over free-text placeholders.
  • Save shared data in tenant fields and matter-specific data in case fields.
  • Before filing, perform one final assembled output review in the destination format.

topic.title: Getting Started (Legal Team)
topic.summary: A practical first-day setup sequence for attorneys, paralegals, and legal support staff.
topic.keywords: getting started first day onboarding users roles permissions tenant fields cases forms assembled forms
topic.content:
First 30 Minutes Checklist

  1. Complete tenant and user sign-in.
  2. Verify role permissions in Users & Security and layered overrides in Permission Layers.
  3. Set firm-wide values in Tenant Fields (firm name, address blocks, defaults).
  4. Create one test matter in Cases.
  5. Enter matter facts in Case Fields.
  6. Assemble one template in Form Assembly.
  7. Open Assembled Forms to confirm output appears and downloads correctly.

Who Should Do What

Role Recommended Responsibilities Primary Screens
Administrator Set roles, enable settings, maintain security and integrations. Users & Security, Tenant Settings, Logs
Paralegal / Legal Assistant Create matters, maintain factual data, and run assembly workflows. Cases, Case Fields, Documents, Form Assembly
Attorney / Reviewer Review output quality, verify filing details, and approve final drafts. Assembled Forms, Documents, Activity Logs

Data Placement Rules (Avoid Rework)

  • Store reusable firm values in tenant fields (for example, firm address and signature data).
  • Store matter-specific facts in case fields (for example, cause number, opposing party names).
  • Use tokenized templates so updates in fields flow automatically into assembled documents.

New User Pitfalls

  • Mixing tenant-level and case-level data for the same fact, which can produce unexpected replacements.
  • Editing templates without token standards, leading to manual post-assembly edits.
  • Skipping output verification before filing deadlines.

Best practice: use consistent internal field/token naming to reduce drafting risk.

Next: Case Workflow Guide

topic.title: Case Workflow Guide
topic.summary: Matter lifecycle steps for legal teams, from intake through finalized document output.
topic.keywords: case workflow intake output cases case fields documents forms assembled forms logs
topic.content:
Intake To Output Sequence

  1. Access and permissions: confirm your team can access required screens in Users & Security and Permission Layers.
  2. Create the matter: open Cases and create a clearly labeled case record.
  3. Capture facts: maintain matter-specific facts in Case Fields.
  4. Prepare supporting documents: manage matter documents in Case Documents.
  5. Assemble forms: use Form Assembly with the selected case and template.
  6. Validate outputs: review generated files in Assembled Forms.
  7. Confirm audit activity: use Activity Logs when troubleshooting or auditing work.

Recommended Intake Data Standard

Data Category Examples Where To Store
Firm-wide constants Firm name, office address, default signature block Tenant Fields
Matter identity Matter label, cause or docket number, county Cases and Case Fields
Dynamic drafting facts Party names, service details, hearing dates Case Fields and case lists/grids when repeating data is needed

Deadline-Driven Review Workflow

  • Run assembly with the final selected matter immediately before filing.
  • Confirm date, county, cause number, and party fields in the assembled output.
  • If a value is wrong, fix the source field first, then re-assemble.
  • Avoid manual edits in final output when tokenized source data can resolve the issue.

Next: Form Assembly Guide

topic.title: Conflict Checks Guide
topic.summary: How to generate, review, and maintain per-case conflicts.xml entries.
topic.keywords: conflicts conflict checks case_conflicts scan nlp ocr tesseract contacts
topic.content:
Where Conflict Data Lives

Each case stores a dedicated conflicts.xml file under the case folder. Open Case Conflicts to review and manage entries.

What The Scan Uses

  • Document version text and metadata.
  • OCR companion text (including Tesseract output when OCR is used).
  • Linked contacts for the selected case.

Suggested Workflow

  1. Open Case Conflicts and select your case.
  2. Run Scan Selected Case to refresh entities.
  3. Search/filter entries by name, type, or source tag.
  4. Add any manual entries required by firm policy.
  5. Remove false positives before final conflict review.

Search Integration

The Search page includes a built-in Case Conflicts search type. Use it to query conflicts across all active cases.

topic.title: Form Assembly Guide
topic.summary: Drafting and quality-control practices for legal professionals assembling pleadings, notices, and other matter documents.
topic.keywords: form assembly templates token standards quality control filings outputs
topic.content:
Before You Assemble

  1. Select the correct matter in Form Assembly.
  2. Verify matter facts in Case Fields.
  3. Confirm firm defaults in Tenant Fields.
  4. Check that the intended template version is selected from the template context list.

Token Drafting Standards

  • Use explicit token scopes: {{case.*}}, {{tenant.*}}, and {{kv.*}}.
  • Prefer one legal fact per token to improve traceability and reduce editing risk.
  • Use fallback notation when a value may be blank, for example: [case.county/County Pending].
  • For advanced references, use Token Guide and Markup Notation.

Quality Control Checklist

Checkpoint Why It Matters
Party names and roles are correct Prevents service or filing errors.
Cause/docket number and county match the matter record Avoids rejected filings and clerical corrections.
Dates render in the expected format Reduces ambiguity in court-facing documents.
No unresolved tokens remain in output Ensures final documents are ready for review or filing.
Output saved in the matter record Supports repeatability and audit trail review.

If Something Looks Wrong

  1. Identify the incorrect value in the assembled output.
  2. Locate the source field (tenant-level or case-level).
  3. Correct the source data, then run assembly again.
  4. Check Activity Logs if behavior is unexpected.

Best practice: correct source data first, then regenerate; avoid manual final-file fixes whenever possible.

topic.title: Facts Guide (Novice)
topic.summary: Step-by-step guide for building a matter case plan using Claims, Elements, and Facts.
topic.keywords: facts novice case plan claims elements evidence links report
topic.content:
Daily Workflow

  1. Open Facts Case Plan.
  2. Select the matter from the matter dropdown.
  3. Create one or more Claims (root legal theories).
  4. Add Elements under each Claim (what must be proven).
  5. Add Facts under each Element (evidence or assertions).
  6. Link each Fact to the source document, part, version, and page number.
  7. Review the side-tree to confirm structure and coverage.
  8. Regenerate the landscape PDF report after significant updates.

What To Enter At Each Level

LevelPurposeExample
Claim Top-level legal claim for the matter. Breach of Contract
Element Required legal element under the claim. Existence of valid contract
Fact Specific factual support tied to evidence. Signed engagement letter on page 1 of Exhibit A

Beginner Tips

  • Keep Claim and Element titles short; put details in summary/notes fields.
  • Use Fact status and strength to prioritize what still needs proof.
  • Always add page numbers when available for faster review.
  • Use internal notes for user-only strategy comments.

Expert Guide

topic.title: Facts Guide (Expert)
topic.summary: Advanced guidance for API usage, evidence linkage quality, and reporting controls.
topic.keywords: facts expert api evidence linkage report refresh automation quality control
topic.content:
Operational Model

  • Case plan hierarchy is matter-scoped and stored as Claims -> Elements -> Facts.
  • Fact records can link to source document, part, version, and page number.
  • Hierarchy updates auto-refresh a matter-linked, versioned landscape PDF report.
  • Report versions are stored in standard document-part-version storage for auditability.

API Operations

Operation GroupExamples
Hierarchy read facts.tree.get, facts.claims.list, facts.elements.list, facts.facts.list
Hierarchy write facts.claims.create/update, facts.elements.create/update, facts.facts.create/update
Evidence linkage facts.facts.link_document and facts.facts.update document/part/version/page fields
Report control facts.report.refresh

Quality Control Checklist

  • Reject facts with no traceable source linkage for filing-critical elements.
  • Require page number entry when a source version is linked.
  • Use status transitions (unverified -> corroborated -> proven) to drive review queues.
  • Monitor report version growth and archive stale facts when matters evolve.

Automation Recommendations

  • Trigger business processes on facts lifecycle events for team notifications.
  • Use facts.tree.get snapshots for review packets and external system syncs.
  • Schedule periodic facts.report.refresh for high-velocity matters.

topic.title: Tasks Guide (Novice)
topic.summary: Step-by-step guide for creating, assigning, and tracking matter work using Tasks.
topic.keywords: tasks novice assignments due date estimates sub-tasks notes reports
topic.content:
Daily Workflow

  1. Open Tasks.
  2. Create a new task with a clear title and description.
  3. Set a required due date/time and required time estimate.
  4. Assign one or more users manually, or use round-robin mode for balancing.
  5. Link the task to matter facts, documents/parts/versions/pages, and/or a thread when relevant.
  6. Add sub-tasks under the parent task for detailed execution planning.
  7. Use internal notes for strategy and team-only communication.
  8. Review assignment history and refresh the task PDF report as needed.

What Makes A Good Task

FieldRecommendationExample
Title Use action-first wording and specific scope. Draft Motion To Compel (Rule 192)
Due Date/Time Enter the legal or internal deadline precisely. 2026-04-10 17:00 local
Time Estimate Use realistic planning minutes for balancing. 120
Associations Link to facts/documents/threads so context is one click away. Fact F2.3, Exhibit B page 5, Thread intake-2026-04

Beginner Tips

  • Use one task per deliverable, then add sub-tasks for discrete steps.
  • Keep due times explicit to reduce missed deadlines.
  • Use internal notes instead of overloading the main description field.
  • Capture custom task attributes for repeatable reporting and automation.

Expert Guide

topic.title: Tasks Guide (Expert)
topic.summary: Advanced guidance for task API usage, assignment balancing, BPM automation, and reporting.
topic.keywords: tasks expert api round robin balancing bpm task fields attributes report refresh
topic.content:
Operational Model

  • Tasks support multi-user assignment, assignment history, and optional round-robin selection.
  • Each task requires due date/time and estimate minutes for scheduling clarity.
  • Tasks can be linked to matter facts (claim/element/fact), document references (doc/part/version/page), and threads.
  • Task updates can regenerate a matter-linked PDF report stored in document version history.

API Operations

Operation GroupExamples
Task records tasks.list, tasks.get, tasks.create, tasks.update, tasks.set_archived
Task notes/assignments tasks.notes.list, tasks.notes.add, tasks.assignments.list
Task custom data task.attributes.list/save, task.fields.get/update
Balancing/reporting tasks.round_robin.next_assignee, tasks.report.refresh

BPM Action Extensions

  • set_task_field: reversible task custom-field mutation with undo/redo support.
  • update_task: update task metadata, assignments, scheduling, and associations.
  • add_task_note: append internal task notes from automation flows.
  • update_fact and refresh_facts_report: automate fact maintenance/report refresh alongside task workflows.
  • See Business Processes (Expert) for shared legal actions such as conflict checks, deadline rules, filing/service, fee verification, and escalation.

Quality Control Checklist

  • Reject creation/update payloads that omit due date/time or estimate minutes.
  • Keep association references valid (fact and document references must resolve).
  • Use assignment reasons for reassignments to improve downstream audit review.
  • Review task PDF version history for critical matter milestones.

topic.title: Business Processes Guide (Expert)
topic.summary: Shared BPM action patterns for legal workflows, including assignment-aware human review gates, branching, and loop-friendly outcomes.
topic.keywords: business processes bpm expert human review branching loops legal actions escalation audit snapshot aging alarms
topic.content:
Human Review Assignment Model

  • Use assignee_user_uuid to route a review to one specific user.
  • Use assignee_role_uuid to route a review to users in one role.
  • If no assignee is set, the review is permission-queue based; users with queue_permission_key can view/act.
  • If queue_permission_key is not set, BPM defaults to business_process_reviews.manage.
  • Use review input keys (for example decision, signature_status) to branch/loop with step conditions.

Shared Legal Actions

ActionRequiredPrimary Output/Outcome Variables
human_reviewtitlevars.last_human_review_uuid, review input under review.*
request_informationtitleWaits for structured response keys (review.*)
document_assemblymatter_uuid, template_uuidvars.last_assembly_uuid, vars.last_document_assembly_status
send_for_signatureto, subjectvars.last_signature_notice_*, optional human-review wait
conflict_checkmatter_uuidvars.last_conflict_status, vars.last_conflict_count
deadline_rule_calculationdays_offsetvars.last_deadline_value (+ optional case field write)
court_filing_servicematter_uuid, titlevars.last_court_filing_task_uuid, optional review gate
payment_fee_verificationrequired_amountvars.last_payment_status, required/paid/delta values
notice_communicationto, subjectvars.last_notice_email_uuid, delivery status vars
wait_for_external_eventtitleWaits until external status is entered in review input
escalationmatter_uuid, titlevars.last_escalation_task_uuid, optional notice queue id
audit_snapshot(none)vars.last_audit_snapshot_hash + activity log record

Branching And Loops

  • Set step conditions to read review.* inputs after any waiting review action.
  • Route REQUEST_CHANGES-style outcomes back to drafting/data steps by setting higher/lower step order branches.
  • Use trigger_event for cross-process loops or event fan-out after review completion.
  • Use on_error=continue only where partial completion is acceptable and audited.

Contact Change Triggers

  • Use contact.created, contact.updated, contact.trashed, and contact.restored for targeted paths.
  • Use contact.changed to catch all contact lifecycle updates in one trigger.
  • Use contact.source_upserted to react specifically to external sync updates.
  • Common payload keys: contact_uuid, display_name, source, source_display, source_contact_id, updated_at, action, and email/phone/address fields.

Configuration Notes

  • Use API operation bpm.actions.catalog as the source of truth for required/optional settings.
  • For email-backed actions (notice_communication, send_for_signature, escalation notices), configure tenant email provider first.
  • Optional aging alarms are configured in Run Health Inspector and email tenant administrators when runs become stale/incomplete.
  • Aging alarm settings keys: bpm_aging_alarm_enabled, bpm_aging_alarm_interval_minutes, bpm_aging_alarm_running_stale_minutes, bpm_aging_alarm_review_stale_minutes, and bpm_aging_alarm_max_issues_per_scan.
  • Office 365 one-way contact sync is configured via tenant settings keys office365_contacts_sync_enabled, office365_contacts_sync_interval_minutes, and office365_contacts_sync_sources_json.
  • For document assembly, pass value.*/values.* and override.*/overrides.* settings when matter defaults need runtime overrides.
  • Record legal-control checkpoints with audit_snapshot before/after critical steps (filing, service, settlement updates).

topic.title: Threads Guide (Novice)
topic.summary: Practical first-use guide for omnichannel Threads (SMS/MMS plus email channels).
topic.keywords: threads novice omnichannel sms mms email attachments assignments manifest report
topic.content:
Daily Thread Workflow

  1. Open Omnichannel Threads.
  2. Create a new thread with the correct channel and linked matter.
  3. Set status, priority, reminder, due date, and assignment.
  4. Add external messages to preserve customer communication history.
  5. Add internal notes for staff-only strategy and follow-up items.
  6. Upload media and files (MMS/email attachments) as evidence.
  7. Regenerate the matter PDF report after key updates.
  8. Use the manifest page to verify embedded multimedia and inclusion status.

What Goes Where

Content TypeWhere To Enter ItVisibility
Customer-facing message Add External Message Included in thread history and report content.
Internal legal/team note Add Internal Note Visible to users; excluded from external-facing report content.
MMS or attachment file Add Attachment / Media Embedded in report unless linked to an internal-only note.

Assignment Basics

  • Use manual mode for direct staffing decisions.
  • Use round-robin mode to balance intake load across users.
  • Use multi-user assignment to coordinate attorney + support staff coverage.
  • Record assignment reasons so the timeline explains why responsibility changed.

Expert Guide

topic.title: Threads Guide (Expert)
topic.summary: Advanced operational guidance for automation, API use, assignment policy, and BPM orchestration.
topic.keywords: threads expert automation api assignments bpm report integrity manifest security
topic.content:
Automation Patterns

  • Use API operations under omnichannel.* for low-latency ingestion and workflow updates.
  • Use bpm.actions.catalog to dynamically build process editors or validation rules.
  • Trigger BPM from thread lifecycle events (omnichannel.thread.created, ...updated, ...attachment.added).
  • Use update_thread and add_thread_note BPM step actions for policy enforcement.
  • Use shared legal actions from Business Processes (Expert) when thread events require cross-team review, escalation, or outbound notices.

Report Integrity Model

  • Thread updates regenerate a matter-linked PDF report with document/part/version history.
  • Public attachments are embedded as PDF attachments and image pages when image media is present.
  • Internal-note-linked media is excluded from embedded external report content.
  • The manifest page should be used as the canonical embedded-content audit surface.

Assignment Policy Recommendations

Policy GoalRecommended Configuration
Balanced intake load Set assignment_mode=round_robin and provide a stable queue candidate list.
Attorney + support co-ownership Use multi-user assignment values and require assignment reason on re-assignment.
SLA tracking Enforce reminder_at/due_at on create/update via BPM rules.

Operational Safety Checklist

  • Never expose raw API secrets in scripts, logs, or exported payloads.
  • Use API capability discovery before deploying automation changes.
  • Run targeted tests after BPM/action changes and API expansion.
  • Validate report generation against threads with attachments and internal notes.

topic.title: Permissions Guide
topic.summary: Reference for Controversies permission layers, group memberships, profiles, and custom object permission keys.
topic.keywords: permissions roles groups users profiles tenant admin custom object access keys security
topic.content:
Permission Resolution Order

Priority Layer Behavior
1 (base) Tenant-level permissions Default permission values applied tenant-wide.
2 Role permissions Role map from Users & Security overlays tenant defaults.
3 Group permissions Enabled groups that include the user override tenant and role values.
4 (highest) User-level permissions User overrides take final precedence over all lower layers.
Special tenant_admin=true Unlimited access to all features regardless of other values.

Managing Permission Layers

  1. Open Permission Layers.
  2. Set tenant-level defaults for broad access policy.
  3. Create groups and assign users to those groups.
  4. Apply permission profiles to tenant, group, or user to accelerate setup.
  5. Add or remove targeted key overrides where needed.
  6. Use user-level overrides only for exceptions, because they override all lower layers.

Profiles and Existing Features

  • Tenant Administrator: full control using tenant_admin=true.
  • Legacy Standard User: mirrors historical non-admin access behavior.
  • Case Operations: case workflow focus (cases, tasks, documents, forms, facts).
  • Review-Only: read-focused access for review workflows.
  • Security Manager: users, roles, permission layers, and logs.
  • Platform Manager: settings, attributes, custom object definitions, and plugin/business process administration.
  • Custom Object Operator: published custom object record operations.

Custom Object Permission Keys

Scope Pattern Example
Generic record access custom_objects.records.<action> custom_objects.records.edit
Per-object access custom_object.<object_key>.<action> custom_object.billing_entry.archive
Definition management custom_objects.manage Access custom object and custom attribute configuration pages.

Actions are: access, create, edit, archive, and export.

Operational Recommendations

  • Use tenant-level values for broad defaults.
  • Use groups for team-based exceptions instead of editing users one-by-one.
  • Reserve user overrides for short-lived exceptions or emergency access changes.
  • Test role/profile changes using a non-admin test account before production rollout.

Open Permission Layers
